# Let's load the Quranic verses JSON file and search for the phrase.
import json, re, unicodedata, sys, os, math, statistics
qfile = "/mnt/data/file-HHumqWkCbpLvx5yFcuNaEH-quranic_verses.json"
with open(qfile, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    qdata = json.load(f)
len(qdata)

hfile = "/mnt/data/file-GTpsjEG93EG6BAuDqWqgAJ-six_hadith_books.json"
with open(hfile, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    hdata = json.load(f)
len(hdata)

# Implement normalization and search.
import re, unicodedata
def normalize_ar(s):
    if s is None:
        return ''
    # ensure string
    s = str(s)
    # remove tatweel
    s = s.replace('\u0640', '')
    # remove tashkeel diacritics (short vowels and marks)
    # Arabic diacritics range includes: 064B-0652, 0660? Expand to common combining marks
    s = re.sub(r'[\u0610-\u061A\u064B-\u065F\u06D6-\u06ED\u0670\u06E0-\u06E1\u06EA-\u06EC\u06EF\u06F0-\u06F9]', '', s)
    # unify hamza variants: map أ إ آ ؤ ئ to ا or maybe to ء; choose ا for base.
    s = re.sub('[إأآٱ]', 'ا', s)
    s = re.sub('[ؤئء]', 'ء', s)
    # remove punctuation (Arabic and common)
    s = re.sub(r'[\u0600-\u06FF&&[^\u0621-\u063A\u0641-\u064A\u0660-\u0669]]', '', s)  # attempt to remove non-letter Arabic symbols - but this regex syntax is not supported
    # Simpler: remove punctuation by allowed chars: keep Arabic letters, numbers, and hamza and spaces
    s = re.sub(r'[^ء\u0621-\u063A\u0641-\u064A\u0660-\u0669\s]', ' ', s)
    # collapse spaces
    s = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', s).strip()
    # lower? not applicable
    return s

# Quick test normalization
q = "وَمَنْ يَتَّقِ اللَّهَ يَجْعَلْ لَهُ مَخْرَجًا وَيَرْزُقْهُ مِنْ حَيْثُ لَا يَحْتَسِ"
print(normalize_ar(q))

# Precompute normalized ayah_text for qdata
for entry in qdata[:5]:
    entry['norm'] = normalize_ar(entry.get('ayah_text',''))

# find candidates whose normalized contains the normalized query
query = "وَمَنْ يَتَّقِ اللَّهَ يَجْعَعْلْ" # intentional typo to test
print(query, '->', normalize_ar(query)[:100])

# Build normalized map for all qdata entries
for entry in qdata:
    if 'norm' not in entry:
        entry['norm'] = normalize_ar(entry.get('ayah_text',''))

query_full = "وَمَنْ يَتَّقِ اللَّهَ يَجْعَلْ لَهُ مَخْرَجًا وَيَرْزُقْهُ مِنْ حَيْثُ لَا يَحْتَسِ"
query_snippet = "وَمَنْ يَت...ا يَحْتَسِ"  # user-provided snippet
nq_full = normalize_ar(query_full)
nq_snip = normalize_ar(query_snippet)

# Find candidates where normalized contains query normalized as substring
candidates = []
for i,entry in enumerate(qdata):
    if nq_full and nq_full in entry['norm']:
        candidates.append((i,entry['ayah_text'],entry['norm']))
len(candidates), candidates[:5]

query_full2 = "وَمَنْ يَتَّقِ اللَّهَ يَجْعَلْ لَهُ مَخْرَجًا وَيَرْزُقْهُ مِنْ حَيْثُ لَا يَحْتَسِبُ"
nq_full2 = normalize_ar(query_full2)
# find candidates containing normalized substring
candidates2 = []
for i,entry in enumerate(qdata):
    if nq_full2 in entry['norm']:
        candidates2.append((i,entry['ayah_text'], entry['norm']))
len(candidates2), candidates2[:5]

part1 = "وَمَنْ يَتَّقِ اللَّهَ يَجْعَلْ لَهُ مَخْرَجًا"
part2 = "وَيَرْزُقْهُ مِنْ حَيْثُ لَا يَحْتَسِبُ"
n1 = normalize_ar(part1)
n2 = normalize_ar(part2)
# find entries where 'norm' equals or contains n1 or n2
matches1 = [(i, e['ayah_text']) for i,e in enumerate(qdata) if n1 in e['norm']]
matches2 = [(i, e['ayah_text']) for i,e in enumerate(qdata) if n2 in e['norm']]
len(matches1), matches1[:5], len(matches2), matches2[:5]

# Let's find the exact substring inside entry 5218
s5218 = qdata[5218]['ayah_text']
s5219 = qdata[5219]['ayah_text']

pattern = "وَمَنْ يَتَّقِ اللَّهَ يَجْعَلْ لَهُ مَخْرَجًا"
# But the actual DB uses doubled tashkeel on certain letters: let's search for normalized pattern inside normalized s5218
def find_substring_exact(entry_text, query):
    # find the exact substring query inside entry_text (case sensitive)
    idx = entry_text.find(query)
    return idx

idx1 = find_substring_exact(s5218, "وَمَنْ يَتَّقِ اللَّهَ يَجْعَلْ لَهُ مَخْرَجًا")
idx1, s5218[idx1: idx1+len("وَمَنْ يَتَّقِ اللَّهَ يَجْعَلْ لَهُ مَخْرَجًا")]

idx2 = find_substring_exact(s5219, "وَيَرْزُقْهُ مِنْ حَيْثُ لَا يَحْتَسِبُ")
idx2, s5219[idx2: idx2+len("وَيَرْزُقْهُ مِنْ حَيْثُ لَا يَحْتَسِبُ")]
s5218[idx1-10: idx1+len("وَمَنْ يَتَّقِ اللَّهَ يَجْعَلْ لَهُ مَخْرَجًا")+10]

seg1 = s5218[idx1: idx1+len("وَمَنْ يَتَّقِ اللَّهَ يَجْعَلْ لَهُ مَخْرَجًا")]
seg2 = s5219[idx2: idx2+len("وَيَرْزُقْهُ مِنْ حَيْثُ لَا يَحْتَسِبُ")]
candidate = seg1 + " " + seg2
candidate, len(candidate)

# Compute token-level F1 similarity
def token_f1(a, b):
    # a and b are normalized strings (no diacritics)
    ta = a.split()
    tb = b.split()
    from collections import Counter
    ca = Counter(ta)
    cb = Counter(tb)
    common = sum((ca & cb).values())
    if common == 0:
        return 0.0
    prec = common / sum(cb.values())
    rec = common / sum(ca.values())
    f1 = 2*prec*rec/(prec+rec)
    return f1, prec, rec, ca, cb

norm_candidate = normalize_ar(candidate)
norm_fullspan = normalize_ar(query_full) # this was truncated
norm_fullspan2 = normalize_ar("وَمَنْ يَتَّقِ اللَّهَ يَجْعَلْ لَهُ مَخْرَجًا وَيَرْزُقْهُ مِنْ حَيْثُ لَا يَحْتَسِ")
print("norm candidate:", norm_candidate)
print("norm fullspan:", norm_fullspan)

f1,prec,rec,_,_ = token_f1(norm_candidate, norm_fullspan)
f1, prec, rec
